sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The batch of 5 clicks all executed but only the last one had gameplay_changed=False. This means after the first click moved the magenta square, the remaining 4 clicks were at the old position (52, 47) which no longer had the magenta square. I need to track the magenta square's position and click on its NEW position each time.
Let me check where the magenta square is now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in sorted(seg, key=lambda x: (x['boundary'][0][0], x['boundary'][0][1])):
    if n['pixels'] >= 3 and n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c']:
        print(f"Color: {n['color']}, pixels: {n['pixels']}, TL: ({n['boundary'][0][0]}, {n['boundary'][0][1]}), children: {n['children']}")

# Check where magenta square is now
magenta = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4]
for m in magenta:
    print(f"Magenta at: {m['boundary'][0]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The magenta square moved from (52, 47) to (51, 46) - only one step despite 5 clicks. This confirms that only the first click worked (the magenta was at 52, 47), and the remaining 4 clicks were wasted because the magenta had already moved to (51, 46).
So I need to click one at a time, updating the position each time. The magenta is now at (51, 46) and the grabber is at (34, 39). The distance is 17 rows and 7 cols. At one step per click, that's about 17 more clicks. Plus I've already used 59 actions.
This is very inefficient. Let me think about whether there's a faster way.
Actually wait - maybe I should click on the grabber instead of the magenta square. Earlier I noticed that clicking on the grabber at (35, 40) caused the blob to shrink from 69 to 60. Maybe clicking on the grabber extends it toward the magenta square?
Let me try a different approach: click on the grabber to extend it toward the magenta square, rather than moving the magenta square toward the grabber.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Magenta moved from (52,47) to (51,46) - only 1 step despite 5 clicks
# Each click only moves it 1 step. This is too slow.
# Let me try clicking on the purple grabber to extend it toward the magenta square
# Grabber is at (34, 39), magenta at (51, 46)
# Click on the grabber
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 35, 'col': 40}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
